text: This exercise is ring roller coaster one. It is one of several ring roller coaster exercises featured in our curriculum and focuses primarily on robotic wrist articulation. The exercise requires the two rings travel around a metal pipe from one end to the other following a tortuous course.

In our experience, we have found that the best way to quickly move the rings is by using only the right hand, as it allows for maximal range of motion while simultaneously moving the ring in the right to left direction.

The surgeon demonstrates robotic wrist articulation by manipulating a ring along a metal pipe. The right hand is pronated with a straight grasper when grabbing the ring, supinated over horizontal or upper curves, pronated while moving upwards, and supinated while moving downwards.

The second ring is maneuvered along the metal pipe using robotic wrist articulation, with the hand pronated while moving upwards, supinated around curves, and alternating between pronation and supination to complete the course.

Keys to scoring high on this exercise require minimizing touching the pipe and excessive force, avoiding dropping the rings, maintaining good vision, and moving efficiently through the course as time is against you.
